name: Non-LLM Constructed-Response Augmentation Update
summary: Stages older and non-LLM examples where synthetic, transformed, manual, feature-space, or image-augmented constructed-response data supplement sparse or imbalanced automated-scoring datasets.

scope.research_question: Are there older or non-LLM examples where generated, transformed, manually simulated, feature-space, or image-augmented constructed-response data supplement sparse or imbalanced automated-scoring datasets, and what role did that synthetic data play in the research process?

proposed_changes.summary: Add the targeted search protocol for non-LLM constructed-response augmentation.
proposed_changes.rationale: The update is based on a targeted search pass and screening decisions should be reviewable.

proposed_changes.summary: Add Lun et al. source metadata.
proposed_changes.rationale: This is a direct older/non-LLM short-answer scoring augmentation example.

proposed_changes.summary: Add Bonthu et al. source metadata.
proposed_changes.rationale: This is a direct programming ASAG text-augmentation example, currently abstract-level.

proposed_changes.summary: Add Krisnawati et al. source metadata.
proposed_changes.rationale: This is a direct short-answer grading example using manual response simulation and SMOTE.

proposed_changes.summary: Add Li, Haudek, and Krajcik source metadata.
proposed_changes.rationale: This is adjacent constructed-response evidence involving scientific-model scoring, SMOTE, and image augmentation.

proposed_changes.summary: Add Lun et al. study artifact.
proposed_changes.rationale: The artifact captures the pre-GPT augmentation strategy, sample, labels, and scorer-training role.

proposed_changes.summary: Add Bonthu et al. study artifact.
proposed_changes.rationale: The artifact records programming ASAG augmentation role while preserving abstract-only limitations.

proposed_changes.summary: Add Krisnawati et al. study artifact.
proposed_changes.rationale: The artifact records manual response augmentation, SMOTE, and cross-prompt ASAG evaluation.

proposed_changes.summary: Add Li et al. study artifact.
proposed_changes.rationale: The artifact records scientific-model scoring, SMOTE/image augmentation, and feature-space boundary conditions.

proposed_changes.summary: Add finding on older/non-LLM short-answer augmentation.
proposed_changes.rationale: The finding verifies that sparse-data supplementation predates recent LLM work.

proposed_changes.summary: Add finding on programming ASAG text augmentation.
proposed_changes.rationale: The finding adds a direct ASAG augmentation example with abstract-only caveats.

proposed_changes.summary: Add finding on manual and SMOTE ASAG augmentation.
proposed_changes.rationale: The finding verifies class-balancing use in a short-answer grading context.

proposed_changes.summary: Add finding on SMOTE/image augmentation for scientific-model scoring.
proposed_changes.rationale: The finding adds adjacent non-text constructed-response evidence while preserving its boundary.

proposed_changes.summary: Update sparse-data supplementation claim with older/non-LLM constructed-response examples.
proposed_changes.rationale: The new evidence broadens the baseline beyond recent LLM and essay-oriented examples.

proposed_claim_implications.note: Expands the existing sparse-data supplementation claim with non-LLM and non-essay examples.

next_actions: Publication complete. Review downstream pages if this change affects shared surfaces.
applicability_boundary: Keep operational-use claims in separate policy/practice nodes.
construct_mapping: Maintain the response-origin and synthetic-data-form distinction in downstream synthesis text.
method_classification: Keep generation_model and scorer_use fields separate in synthesis exports.
scorer_validation_relevance: Retain the distinction between training augmentation and scorer validation in the literature review.
search_protocol: Leave systematicity_level as targeted_baseline_review and keep screening counts non-exhaustive.
source_fidelity: Retrieve full text before using Bonthu et al. for detailed method comparison or quantitative synthesis.
source_fidelity: Use Li, Haudek, and Krajcik in all source, finding, and synthesis text.
synthesis_overreach: Use language such as targeted pass, examples, or pattern rather than systematic prevalence claims.
Resolve 1 structured source-access follow-up action(s) before synthesis use.
Resolve 1 structured extraction follow-up action(s) before quantitative synthesis.

source_access_follow_up_actions.reason: create-finding-bonthu: finding bonthu-non-llm-asag-augmentation-finding-2023 relies on Improving the Performance of Automatic Short Answer Grading Using Transfer Learning and Augmentation with abstract only, below the domain minimum of full_text.
source_access_follow_up_actions.recommended_action: Retrieve full text for Improving the Performance of Automatic Short Answer Grading Using Transfer Learning and Augmentation, replace or downgrade the finding, or mark the source as screening-only before synthesis use.

extraction_follow_up_actions.reason: finding bonthu-non-llm-asag-augmentation-finding-2023 reports metric text but numeric_results_extracted is false.
extraction_follow_up_actions.recommended_action: Extract table-level numeric results for Non-LLM Text Augmentation for Programming ASAG before quantitative synthesis.
